review_report.object_summary: stock-picking S3 Design baseline + P0 schema freeze + registry baseline + execution-guard skeleton + migration plan (M3.5 review gate)

ralph_loop_entry_conditions.condition: GRV Battle round 2 has no blocker
DESIGN.md defines module boundaries, directory structure, call order, failure behavior

ralph_loop_entry_conditions.evidence: 16 P0 schema names frozen; 3 silent drifts from REQ-01 (M001-M003) require M4a Week 1 close
registry.yaml has taroc + chokepoint + maturity_gate; registry-design.md has 6-step snapshot atomicity + 14 reject codes + 11 tests

ralph_loop_entry_conditions.condition: execution-guard refusal tests written into S3
ralph_loop_entry_conditions.evidence: execution-guard.md has 7 refusal tests (exceeds 5 required by GRV) + observable signals
this report: WARN with M001-M003 closing in M4a

battle_round2_absorption.topic: Chokepoint exit criteria AND logic + thesis break definition
battle_round2_absorption.evidence: DESIGN §3.5 AND logic + Major thesis break definition 3 条

battle_round2_absorption.topic: custom_ref whitelist location, load timing, audit path
battle_round2_absorption.evidence: DESIGN §3.4 + registry-design.md §Custom Ref Whitelist + custom_refs.yaml + DESIGN Decision Log 2026-06-24
execution-guard.md §Observable Signals 'Broker API call count for rejected paths must be zero or provably blocked'

battle_round2_absorption.topic: validation idempotency must not depend on mutable signal_date
battle_round2_absorption.evidence: data-schema.md validation_event.v1 idempotency key is (draft_id, validation_run_id, calendar_checked_at, validation_session_key) — stronger than R2's either/or suggestion

findings.description: data-schema simplifies evidence_id to plain uuid, dropping REQ-01's explicit ev_<ulid> type prefix. No Decision Log entry, no migration note. Type prefix is useful for audit/index/grep to distinguish evidence from other uuid entities (candidate_id, draft_id, approval_id).
findings.recommendation: Add ## Schema Changelog section in data-schema.md, decide whether to retain ev_ prefix. If yes, revert data-schema to ev_<ulid> (or string with regex). If no, document the rationale.

findings.description: REQ-01 has 5 values (primary/secondary/community/ai_inference/unverified); data-schema extends to 9 values (adds regulatory/broker_data/company_filing/news/analyst/internal_note, removes community/unverified). This is both extension and rename. The community+unverified merge to analyst loses 'unverified' semantics.
findings.evidence: REQ-01: 'source_type: primary | secondary | community | ai_inference | unverified' / data-schema: 'source_type: primary | secondary | regulatory | broker_data | company_filing | news | analyst | internal_note | ai_inference'
findings.recommendation: In schema changelog: (a) document 5 new values with REQ-01 §节点13 cross-references, (b) explicitly declare community+unverified merge to analyst (preserve 'unverified' as source_quality=unknown), or (c) keep all 11 values.

findings.description: Two irreversible simplifications: (a) publisher_authority+ai_classified_quality+classification_method 3 fields disappear, merged into single source_quality enum — loses dual-track scoring (publisher objective + AI subjective), future weighted quality requires schema change; (b) claim.v1 completely rewritten: REQ-01 has claim_kind+claim_subtype+polarity+valid_until, data-schema has claim_type (4 enum) + thesis_broken boolean — flattens 'two-dimensional' (kind+polarity) into 'four-quadrant enum' (positive/negative/break_condition/inference), downstream tracking/weekly-review cannot distinguish 'known risk in positive thesis' (was polarity=positive + claim_kind=risk).
findings.evidence: REQ-01 §节点13 evidence_ref.v1 + claim.v1 (longer field set) / data-schema (much shorter)
findings.recommendation: In schema changelog explicitly declare: (a) whether publisher_authority+ai_classified_quality are needed — if yes, restore number fields or use extensions; (b) claim.v1 at minimum restore claim_kind+polarity, or use claim_type+claim_subtype. Must close in M4a Week 1 schema validator phase.

findings.description: REQ-01 risk_event.v1 source enum has 4 values; data-schema adds strategy_tracking. event_type enum from 5 values adds thesis_broken. thesis_broken is justified by DESIGN §3.5 Chokepoint thesis break definition, but strategy_tracking is undeclared anywhere, and thesis_broken is already expressible in tracking_event.v1 event_type — redundancy.
findings.evidence: REQ-01: 'source: position_monitor | reconcile | portfolio_risk | execution_guard' / data-schema: 'source: ... | strategy_tracking' + 'event_type: ... | thesis_broken'
findings.recommendation: In schema changelog: (a) strategy_tracking source = tracking_event promoting thesis_broken=true to risk_event, or (b) remove to avoid tracking_event.v1 duplication.

findings.description: data-schema.md lists legacy_csv_projection.v1 in Compatibility section; migration-plan.md does not reference this schema name. Loose coupling.
findings.evidence: data-schema.md L5: 'Compatibility: legacy_csv_projection.v1' / migration-plan.md: no reference

findings.location: data-schema.md strategy_dispatch.v1.output_schema vs registry.yaml Chokepoint output_schema
findings.description: data-schema allows output_schema: draft_candidates.v1 | theme_research.v1. registry.yaml has TAROC output_schema=draft_candidates.v1, Chokepoint output_schema=theme_research.v1. Consistent for static view, but Chokepoint v1 upgrades to draft on evidence threshold (REQ-01 §节点4) — same strategy at different stages has different output_schema. registry field cannot express this runtime multi-schema.
findings.evidence: data-schema strategy_dispatch.v1.output_schema 双值 / registry.yaml 单值
findings.recommendation: In registry-design.md §Output Schema: Chokepoint output_schema declares 'primary output'; secondary output (draft_candidates.v1) triggered by promotion_status. M4a dispatch output_schema must reflect actual run schema, not registry static field.

findings.description: File exists with refs: [] (empty). Expected for v1, but DESIGN §3.4 custom_ref policy #4 requires whitelist change to be in Decision Log — initial state should be documented in file header.
findings.recommendation: Add header comment: '# Empty: v1 rejects all custom_refs. Any future entry must be approved via design/DESIGN.md Decision Log and reference a registry-approved strategy record or signed internal reference.'

findings.description: migration-plan.md §Audit Fields requires 6 fields per migrated event; does not consider evidence_id format transition between REQ-01 (ev_<ulid>) and data-schema (uuid). If M001 decides to retain ev_ prefix, migration must handle old CSV reason-field evidence_id references (no prefix).
findings.recommendation: Add sub-step in migration-plan.md §Migration Sequence step 3: 'Normalize evidence_id format and document any id-prefix transition in migration_audit'. Sync with M001.

findings.description: data-schema.md has no §Verification section defining executable acceptance points for M4a schema validator implementation. M4a needs to know what 'schema freeze' means in testable terms.
findings.recommendation: Add §Verification: M4a must produce (a) JSON Schema / Pydantic / Zod as executable validator, (b) validate_schema.py passing positive+negative test cases for all 16 schemas.

findings.description: Document states P0 schema changes must go through Decision Log after S3 freeze, but data-schema.md has no changelog section, and DESIGN.md Decision Log has 4 non-schema entries. M001-M003 reveal schema changes have already occurred but not logged.
findings.evidence: data-schema.md no changelog / DESIGN.md Decision Log all non-schema
findings.recommendation: Add ## Schema Changelog section at top of data-schema.md. M4a Week 1: write M001-M003 dispositions there.

findings.description: 6 UX paths defined (proceed/skip/needs_override/fail/reject/execution_blocked). No distinction between idempotency_key replay (cached result) and new run.
findings.recommendation: Add UX rule: 'idempotency_key replay returns cached result (with warnings), does not re-execute.'

findings.description: Specifies broker API call count signal, but no concrete implementation hook. Three paths (decorator/proxy/monkey patch) all workable.
findings.recommendation: Add §Implementation Hook: recommend method decorator @execution_guard wrapping futu_tool.buy/longbridge.place_order; guard block raises ExecutionBlockedError; counter accumulated by decorator. M4a scope.

findings.description: 4 acceptance criteria include 'idempotent rerun' but no explicit 'cold start vs warm restart behavior consistency' regression test.
findings.recommendation: Add acceptance 5: 'Migration can resume from a partially completed batch without duplicating events; restart-after-failure must be idempotent.'

risks.description: DESIGN §3.3 says validator must preserve field names and reject unknown critical enums, but data-schema already silently drifted from REQ-01 §节点13 field set. If validator implements against current data-schema, it freezes REQ-01 §节点13 review conclusions as outdated version.
risks.mitigation: M4a schema validator implementation must first do REQ-01 ↔ data-schema reconciliation; M001-M003 close in M4a Week 1

risks.description: registry-design.md states 'S4 can implement copy-on-read in single process; no filesystem transaction required for v1 as long as selector never mixes two snapshots' — this is developer discipline, not system constraint. custom_refs.yaml + registry.yaml simultaneous load atomicity also lacks lock.
risks.mitigation: M4a use process-level singleton + bytes-after-read hash verification; v1.1 add file lock if concurrency needed

risks.description: registry.yaml maturity_gate is data-complete, but no module is responsible for 'how many manual runs this week / any thesis_broken events this week'. Gate is data, evaluator is missing.
risks.mitigation: M4b implement evaluator in position-monitor or new chokepoint-maturity-evaluator module; M3.5 only validates data shape

risks.description: futu_tool.py buy fix is code-side; execution-guard.md §Observable Signals gives acceptance signal not implementation mechanism. Decorator/proxy/monkey patch all workable.
risks.mitigation: M4a Week 1 decorator pattern first; keep broker client method names unchanged

risks.description: migration-plan.md only says 'keep legacy CSV read-only during compatibility window', doesn't say 'after reader switches to event store, whether CSV projection is still generated'.
risks.mitigation: S5 acceptance explicitly test 'after event store switch, CSV projection still generated and matches new writes'

risks.description: GRV marks evidence store P1, S3 does not put evidence-store module in §3.6 file structure
risks.mitigation: M4b evaluate evidence-store landing timing; if M4b does not touch evidence-store, evidence_ref/claim in data-schema remain dry fields

acceptance_criteria_for_m4a_exit: M001 disposition written in data-schema.md ## Schema Changelog (retain ev_ prefix or backport to plain uuid with rationale)
M002 disposition written in data-schema.md ## Schema Changelog (9-value extended enum or 11-value full set or merge rationale)
M003 disposition written in data-schema.md ## Schema Changelog (publisher_authority+ai_classified_quality restore, claim.v1 kind+polarity restore, or simplify rationale)
validate_schema.py passes positive+negative tests for all 16 P0 schemas
validate_registry.py passes 11 tests from registry-design.md §Required Tests
execution-guard decorator pattern implemented; 7 refusal tests pass as executable
approval-gate skeleton accepts approval.v1 input and emits execution_guard_decision.v1
migration scaffold produces dry-run batch from one legacy CSV; rerun is idempotent
broker API call counter wired and reports zero for blocked paths
data-schema.md ## Schema Changelog section created and M001-M003 dispositions recorded
review_report.most_important_recommendation: M4a Week 1 must do 'REQ-01 ↔ data-schema reconciliation' and close M001-M003 before schema validator implementation. S3 baseline's 'double freeze' is paper freeze — data-schema has already silently drifted from REQ-01 §节点13 evidence/claim field set. If validator implements against current data-schema, it freezes REQ-01 §节点13 review conclusions as outdated version. This is the only real risk at M3.5 gate — other dimensions are engineering effort, this one is 'whether double-freeze principle is self-consistent'.
review_report.rating_rationale: Not PASS because M001-M003 (REQ-01 vs S3 data-schema silent drift) are about S3 baseline vs S2 node review baseline consistency — the root of double-freeze principle. Schema validator implementation depends on these 3 dispositions. Not FAIL/BLOCK because M001-M003 are schema field adjustments, not factual errors, not security holes, not logical jumps; all 3 can close in M4a Week 1 via 1-2 days 'reconciliation + write changelog'. WARN because double-freeze principle is paper-level, other dimensions are engineering-controllable, battle R2 fully absorbed, registry has 7 refusal tests, migration plan has 6 audit fields — S3 baseline is overall shippable.
